{{Quote|Take the word of ''Ansible Beelyard''—here you have found a universe untainted by the darkness of the Empire!|Ansible Beelyard|<ref name="marvel46" />}}
'''Ansible Beelyard''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was a well-known hero of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the early days of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Known as the '''Holy Terror''', Beelyard was the right-hand man of the famous [[Cody Sunn-Childe]] in ferocious fights against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] that earned them notoriety across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="marvel46" /> However, around [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Beelyard, Sunn-Childe and the rest of their resistance band disappeared mysteriously—having had a religious revelation after a battle that awoke latent psychic abilities, Sunn-Childe created a [[City of Dreams|dream utopia]] free of war, and there adopted a peaceful way of life for a time along with Beelyard and their companions.<ref name="marvel46" />

Beelyard and Sunn-Childe's whereabouts were unknown until [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], when Rebels [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] arrived in Sunn-Childe's pocket dimension by accident due to a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] malfunction. Their presence attracted the attention of a nearby group of Imperial troops, who began attacking Sunn-Childe's City of Dreams. Rather than forsake their pacifistic philosophy and fight back, Beelyard and his companions allowed the Imperials to destroy Sunn-Childe's realm and [[Death/Legends|kill]] them.<ref name="marvel46" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ansible Beelyard first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 46|''Star Wars'' (1977) 46]], written by [[John Marc DeMatteis]] and released in [[1981]]. In the issue, Beelyard was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel46" />
